prompt: A hyper-realistic geological cross-section of the Earth's crust viewed from the side. The deepest layers show traditional dinosaur fossils and rock strata. However, the top-most geological layer, representing the Anthropocene, is a thick, compressed stratum entirely made of crushed colorful plastic bottles, toys, circuit boards, and synthetic waste, fossilized into a solid block. On the surface above this plastic layer, a single, withered tree struggles to grow, its roots unable to penetrate the synthetic bedrock. The lighting is dramatic and subterranean, highlighting the permanence of the waste. No text, no labels.
dataset_name: Production, use, and fate of all plastics ever made (Geyer et al., 2017)
insight: We have created a permanent geological layer of plastic, the 'Technofossil' stratum, which will persist long after humanity is gone.
